core_mechanism: a latent-reservoir reactivation threshold: a hidden state variable retained during an apparently absorbing quiescent phase acts as a sufficient statistic whose scalar recruitment capacity sets a sharp threshold separating extinction from spontaneous reignition of a new activity cycle
brief_reason: The abstract shows the residual synaptic snapshot at the first silent state, summarized by a scalar Latent Excitatory Recruitment capacity (cumulative fresh excitatory units), near-perfectly predicts whether the system dies after one cycle or spontaneously regenerates another, i.e., a hidden reservoir threshold governing escape from an absorbing state.
